title: pkg/api: api list quadlets resource
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
This PR introduces a new api endpoint to list quadlets.
Closes #27119 

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Add quadlet list resource for api
```

comments.body:
I havent contributed much to podman yet, so Im opening this PR early to get feedback and make sure Im doing it right.

The api in /libpod/quadlets/json is returing

```json
[
  {
    "Name":"nginx.container",
    "UnitName":"nginx.service",
    "Path":"/home/hiaki/.config/containers/systemd/nginx.container",
    "Status":"failed/failed",
    "App":""
  }
]
```

Some questions that I have:
- Does it need to return more data?
- Should it support any params like "filter" in quadlet list --filter?
- Should I add more routes besides /libpod/quadlet/json?

> Does it need to return more data?

It should be fine to return the quadlet list data as is.

> Should it support any params like "filter" in quadlet list --filter?

Yes I think it should accept the filter as query param.


> Should I add more routes besides /libpod/quadlet/json?

Long term it would be good to have API and then podman-remote support for all podman quadlet commands. That however is quite a bit of work so I would consider this out of scope for you now until you really like to take this on.

What is needed for this PR is a simple test to at least make sure the endpoint works. You could create a new test file in test/apiv2 and just call the endpoint for now even if the result will be empty there. Once podman-remote quadlet support is added we can enable the podman quadlet tests in test/system/253-podman-quadlet.bats which should cover it in more detail then.

cc @ninja-quokka @inknos

> Long term it would be good to have API and then podman-remote support for all podman quadlet commands. That however is quite a bit of work so I would consider this out of scope for you now until you really like to take this on.

Agree with this. This is an effort we are currently willing to make, so we could move the discussion for quadlet api upstream to have consistency. I also suggest taking a small step for now

In general, it LGTM.  You'll need to add a test or three before advancing this.
Thanks @nothiaki !
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
@Luap99 I added the query params filter and created a test file with a simple test case and a note.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *nothiaki* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> LGTM, if you need to repush or for future contributions please also always add the `Fixes: #27119` to the commit message not just in the PR description.

Thank you! I will make sure to add the Fixes message in future contributions.
